setup.context: You earned twenty dollars and want to make it grow

root.narration: You earned twenty dollars from chores. Your mom says you have three choices: spend it now on a toy, put it in a jar in your room, or put it in a savings account at the bank where it grows a little each month like planting a seed. What do you choose?

choices.text: Put it in the savings account so it grows over time like a seed growing into a plant
Put it in the jar so you can see it and add more later
Spend it all on a toy right now

outcome.headline: Your money is growing!
outcome.explanation: The bank pays you a little extra money each month just for keeping your money there. It is like planting a seed: you put in twenty dollars, and after a year you have more than twenty dollars without doing any extra work. The longer you leave it, the bigger it grows.
outcome.whatToRemember: Investing means putting your money somewhere it can grow. Like a seed needs time to become a tree, money needs time to grow. The earlier you start, the more it grows.

keyTerms.definition: Putting your money to work so it grows over time. Just like planting a seed and waiting for it to become a big plant, invested money gets bigger the longer you leave it.

outcome.headline: Safe but not growing
outcome.explanation: Your money is safe in the jar and you can watch it pile up as you add more. But it will not grow on its own. A year from now it will still be exactly twenty dollars, not a penny more.
outcome.whatToRemember: Saving in a jar protects your money, but it does not help it grow. A savings account or investment makes your money work for you even while you sleep.

outcome.headline: Gone in one afternoon!
outcome.explanation: The toy was fun for a few days, but now it sits in your closet. If you had invested that twenty dollars and added a little each month, by the time you are older it could have grown into much more.
outcome.whatToRemember: Spending everything means your money stops working. Investing even small amounts when you are young gives your money the most time to grow.
